things.name: Antwerp Cathedral
things.description: Gothic Cathedral of Our Lady with the world's tallest spire housing four Rubens masterpieces including the Descent from the Cross

things.name: Grote Markt
things.description: Stunning medieval market square with the ornate Renaissance town hall and guild houses overlooking the Brabo Fountain

things.name: Rubenshuis
things.description: Former home and studio of Baroque master Peter Paul Rubens, restored to its 17th-century grandeur with original artworks

things.name: Museum aan de Stroom (MAS)
things.description: Striking spiral tower museum tracing Antwerp's history as one of the world's greatest trading ports

things.name: Antwerp Central Station
things.description: Breathtaking Belle Époque railway station of 1905 nicknamed the railway cathedral for its soaring dome and arches

things.name: Het Steen
things.description: Antwerp's oldest building, a medieval fortress on the Scheldt waterfront, now a visitor centre for city tourism

things.name: Royal Museum of Fine Arts
things.description: Magnificent collection of Flemish Primitive and Baroque art including 20 Rubens paintings in a restored neoclassical palace

things.name: Diamond Quarter
things.description: Heart of the global diamond trade with over 1500 companies, jewellery shops and the Antwerp Diamond Museum

things.name: Plantin-Moretus Museum
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage printing house with the world's oldest printing presses and archive of Christophe Plantin's 16th-century empire

things.name: Stadspark
things.description: Elegant Victorian city park with sculpted gardens, ponds and the Royal Academy of Fine Arts, ideal for a relaxing stroll
